programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Andrea's cover has been well and truly blown, leaving her to face the wrath of Gina and fatally compromising her testimony at the trial of rapist Alan Kennedy, who is acquitted. Desperate for a chance to redeem herself, she makes one last effort to gather incriminating evidence against Gabriel - only to end up being held hostage in a store room by the crazed copper. Events come to a head when Colin is dismissed after being exposed as a vicious racist and retaliates by handcuffing Ken in a petrol-doused van and crashing it into Sun Hill's front office. Blissfully unaware of the escalating crisis, Reg calls Marilyn's mobile phone at the station, intending to ask for her hand in marriage - and triggers a massive explosion(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: In a special episode filmed in Ghana, Diane goes in search of Ric to persuade him to return home. She finds him working in his brother's clinic, the only available source of medical care for the local community, which is in desperate need of funding. An outraged Ric demands she take the first flight back to the UK, declaring that he's needed where he is. But Diane is not convinced his reasons for staying are entirely altruistic, and investigates unresolved conflicts in his family concerning the death of his father. As she prepares to return home, an emergency involving a pregnant woman brings their personal and professional dilemmas to a head. Adrian Edmondson guest stars(n)

programme.title: Casualty

programme.title: The Bill

Dale also faces a fiery demise in the burning building, while Jim struggles to accept the news that his friend Ken was killed instantly when the van blew up. Marilyn fights for life in hospital, a grief-stricken Reg at her bedside - and as he tearfully slips an engagement ring onto her finger, an ominous bleep from her cardiac monitor heralds a tragic end to their relationship(n)

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Journalist Nikki Fox teams up with scientists at Liverpool John Moores University to discover what takeaways do to our health, what's really in them and how people can kick the habit. In a unique experiment, 15 volunteers eat nothing but takeaways for two weeks and undergo a barrage of tests, from blood sugar and body fat to gut bacteria and mental sharpness. The results reveal the shocking truth about how takeaways affect our bodies and brains(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A blind actress is the only witness to her husband's murder, but it remains to be seen whether she can really be trusted. Drama, starring Ralf Little, Aude Legastelois and Frances Tomelty(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce hosts the political debate from Middlesbrough, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Chris Mason, Katya Adler and Adam Fleming present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden(n)

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal
programme.desc: Liverpool Council's housing team works on the case of a family whose members left their rented property in an appalling state - and the landlord in shock(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore properties about to be auctioned in Wednesfield, Wolverhampton, Sydenham in south London and Chatham in Kent, then talk to the new owners(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: South African-born Candice Crawford hopes to persuade husband Sean to move to New Zealand permanently after sampling a week in Tauranga. Presented by Nicki Chapman(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Officers search the home of a loan shark and find a safe containing thousands of pounds. The safe proves to be a breakthrough in their investigation into an illegal money lending operation in Dudley in the West Midlands. The loan shark had made hundreds of thousands of pounds by pushing hard-up families further into debt. The enforcers also force a rogue builder to pay compensation to his elderly victims(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Caroline Hawley and Raj Bisram assist the red and blue teams as they look for items of interest and value in Detling, Kent. Presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp monitors proceeding and also visits a nearby farm to lend a hand with the hop harvest(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al looks forward to the night of the lock-in at the Icon, though as soon as he arrives, his expectations are dampened. Jimmi gets a new cellmate who challenges him to a game of cards, with the winner getting to ask a question of the loser(n)

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: Tracking a local conman, Frank and Lu make some unlikely allies when they encounter a pair of deadly gangsters. Mark Benton and Jo Joyner star in the comedy drama about an oddball couple of private detectives who investigate crime in Stratford-upon-Avon(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a couple up sticks from Birmingham and spend £500,000 on a country home that allows them to lead the good life while being close enough to Hereford for regular visits to their daughter. While in Herefordshire, Jules visits a family-run blacksmiths that forges commissioned pieces for historic buildings, and takes to the anvil to make a wrought iron accessory for his own home(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Sometimes taking big risks can make a difference to the farming industry, and here three inspirational farming families are celebrated for their ideas and ingenuity. However, only one can be crowned Diversification Farmer of the Year at the Farmers Weekly Awards. Narrated by Bill Paterson(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and Paul Laidlaw peruse antique shops in Oxfordshire, Gloucestershire, Warwickshire and Northamptonshire as they head to the final auction in Middle Littleton, Worcestershire. Plus, a visit to Shakespeare's birthplace in Stratford-upon-Avon to find out about a 19th-century, Bard-inspired, American black actor(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey host the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Dave and Harry Rich are in Hereford, where they come up with ideas to transform a back garden for a family that has four generations living in the same house. With a limited budget, the designers come up with some ideas for creating garden sculptures and focal points on a shoestring(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: FIFA Women's World Cup Golden Boot winner Ellen White, Great Britain 400m runner Perri Shakes-Drayton, former Everton midfielder Leon Osman and Grand National-winning jockey Mick Fitzgerald join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: As everyone tries to get back to normal following recent events, one person's lies begin to unravel. Meanwhile, Karen inspires Keegan with an idea to cheer up Tiffany, but Keegan's plan ends in disaster(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The first quarter-final sees the six talented heat winners return to fight for a place in Knockout Week. Facing the Critic's Test, the week's best-performing amateurs take on an intimidating task, cooking to a brief set by top food critic Jay Rayner, who asks them to produce an exceptional dish incorporating stuffing. Hosted by John Torode and Gregg Wallace and narrated by India Fisher(n)

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Newlyweds Dermot and Maria argue over whether they should buy their own home. Meanwhile, Grandad hopes to witness his own funeral, but Mrs Brown's plans go awry when she mistakes two Mormons - who have been stealing Father Quinn's parishioners - for the men who are supposed to be delivering the coffin(n)

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: Strictly Come Dancing's AJ Pritchard and his brother Curtis choreograph the three queens as they take on an all-singing, all-dancing final challenge. Judges Michelle Visage, Alan Carr, Graham Norton and the eliminated queens all return as the finalists take to the runway one last time before RuPaul crowns the series' ultimate winner(n)

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: The participants of Drag Race UK watch the final episode for the very first time, awaiting the big 'Ruveal'. Host RuPaul, judges Michelle Visage, Graham Norton and Alan Carr, the three finalists and the celebrated cast of beautiful queens reflect on the highlights of the series(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with the Mudhar family, who spent a trial week in Melbourne, to see if they decided to make the move or stay put in London(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Danny Sebastian and Caroline Hawley help two teams look for items at an antiques centre in King's Lynn, Norfolk, while presenter Eric Knowles meets an unlikely war hero(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores the West Country between the wars, setting off from the Cornish seaside resort of St Ives and ending his first in the former mining village of St Day(n)

programme.title: Yorkshire Walks
programme.desc: Artist Shanaz Gulzar seeks inspiration while walking a stretch of the 109-mile Cleveland Way, meeting fellow walkers along the way and finding spots to sketch and recite poetry. Starting in Runswick Bay, and armed only with a handheld 360-degree selfie-style camera. she climbs the steep steps to the cliff top before strolling along the coastal path, absorbing the beautiful landscape and its industrial past, before finishing at Whitby for sunset(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond(n)

programme.title: Hardball
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Ore Oduba in which contestants have to answer questions to get as far along a track as they can before a ball catches and passes them, ending their go(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Three semi-detached homes in York - including a two-up, two-down 1930s house reinvented for the modern market and a 1970s communal building - are in the spotlight(n)

programme.title: Lorraine's Fast, Fresh and Easy Food
programme.desc: Lorraine Pascale demonstrates simple dishes completely from scratch, ideal for today's hectic world where, for many, packet and microwave meals have become the norm. She has solutions for all occasions, beginning with ideas for easy entertaining when friends or family turn up out of the blue and reveals how it is possible to spend less time in the kitchen and more time having fun with everyone else(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: A corpse's identification reopens a dismissed case. Sharon is offered a transfer, and Ash suffers for her closeness to Supt Dunne(n)

programme.title: The Great British Winter
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison continues her exploration of Britain's most extreme winter landscapes, searching for beauty behind the bleakness within forest environments. She encounters inhabitants including red squirrels and Eurasian beavers, and discovers that during these unforgiving months vital tasks including charcoal creation and tree planting get under way(n)

programme.title: Big Blue UK
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and his team of experts turn their attention to seabirds, and Richard Taylor Jones aims to dispel a popular misconception about 'seagulls'(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: In Morecambe, Lancashire, antiques experts Charles Hanson and Catherine Southon find treasures to take to auction including a vintage Rolex watch. Plus, presenter Paul Martin discovers the rags to riches story of the town's most famous son - Eric Morecambe(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Samira Ahmed, Dom Joly, Angela Rippon and John Robins test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo visits Heaton Park in Manchester, where one of the first open air concerts was held in 1909, before travelling on to the town of Oldham. Michael then ventures to Edale in the Peak District, where he joins ramblers in walking country, and learns how a Sheffield socialist spearheaded a mass trespassing revolt to open up the countryside to working people(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Neil Show
programme.desc: Interviews, discussion and analysis from Westminster, including the latest on Brexit with Andrew Neil and guests(n)

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Lochs
programme.desc: Paul Murton returns to explore more of the nation's lochs, beginning by heading to Wester Ross to find out about the heritage of Little Loch Broom. He then travels to the off-grid community of Scoraig, where he learns about violin making and wind power, before taking the plunge to explore the hidden depths of Corrieshalloch Gorge. In Ullapool, on the shores of Loch Broom, Paul learns the Russian word for parsley, and how a huge fleet of Russian fish factory ships known as Klondykers used to visit the town(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of the unpaid volunteers who sacrifice their free time to staff more than two hundred RNLI lifeboat stations across the UK. The volunteers race to the rescue of a shark attack victim, and two fishermen caught by the tide find themselves stranded in a rising sea. Elsewhere, two young boys are swept out to sea in a rip current(n)

programme.title: Murder 24/7
programme.desc: The investigation into the murder of Courtney Valentine-Brown nears a conclusion. With one suspect outstanding, one on bail and one in custody, can the team of detectives and specialists secure enough evidence to charge them with murder?(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Ed Byrne plays host in this show recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo. He warms up the audience and then introduces routines by Angela Barnes and Geoff Norcott(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark(n)

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: In the final episode, crime investigators use forensics to try to find a suspect after a teenage girl is raped by a stranger in a park in central Newcastle. Alongside the traditional forensic search for body fluids, DNA and fibres, the police also deploy the rapidly evolving skills of their digital forensic team(n)

programme.title: Universal Credit: Inside the Welfare State
programme.desc: Documentary exploring Universal Credit from the point of view of both government employees and those who rely on the controversial benefits system to support themselves. The first episode focuses on Peckham Jobcentre in London. Rachel, who left her job to care for her elderly parents, now struggles to support her family on Universal Credit while having to cope with debt and depression. Jobcentre employee Karen finds herself faced with similar difficulties to her clients, and has to take a second job to support herself. The programme also follows the difficulties faced by civil servants in overseeing sweeping changes to the benefits system(n)

programme.title: Mary Beard's Shock of the Nude
programme.desc: Part two of two. The classicist looks at an eclectic range of works to show how artists have challenged the idea of 'the body beautiful', and explores how artistic nudes can provoke viewers to consider fundamental questions about being human. Mary reflects on Marc Quinn's sculpture of artist Alison Lapper, Sally Mann's images of her own children in the 1990s and the controversy that surrounded their exhibition, and examines society's shifting perception of gender, from the binary of male and female to a more fluid concept(n)

programme.title: Rio and Kate: Becoming a Stepfamily
programme.desc: Documentary following former England captain Rio Ferdinand and his fiancee Kate Wright in the months leading up to their wedding as she integrates into the family and becomes a stepmother to his three children Lorenz, Tate and Tia. Kate takes on not only the role as Rio's new partner, but also of parent to three children still grieving over the death of their mother Rebecca in 2015 and grandmother Janet two years later(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: In Blackburn, Anita Manning values a gold charm bracelet, and Philip Serrell takes a shine to an unusual globe. Paul Martin finds about the life of singer Kathleen Ferrier(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps newlyweds from London find a home in Surrey, and learns about a project at the British Wildlife Centre that is protecting an important native species(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo visits Cornwall's county town, Truro, heading to the historic estate of Trewithen, whose gardens were stocked from China by professional plant hunters commissioned by its owner. The Atlantic Coast branch line carries Michael north to Newquay, where he discovers a pioneering surfer and braves the waves on a belly board. In the nearby village of St Mawgan, Michael is introduced to the ancient Cornish sport of 'wrassling', which surged in popularity between the wars as part of a Cornish Celtic revival(n)

programme.title: Lose Weight and Get Fit with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: After a positive first couple of weeks, the temptation of unhealthy treats and alcohol is threatening to undo the group's progress, so Tom shares a series of recipes to help the volunteers with their temptations for unhealthy treats. He starts with his own take on Asian crispy duck pancakes, before demonstrating how to make fudge chocolate brownies which are lower in calories. Later, Tom visits Lauren in her home, where he gives her a lesson in making prawn and avocado rice paper rolls which contain lots of fresh, healthy ingredients(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond(n)

programme.title: Hardball
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Ore Oduba in which contestants have to answer questions to get as far along a track as they can before a ball catches and passes them, ending their go(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Three wildcard properties are thrown into the mix in York - an eco-friendly futuristic dream home, a converted barn with a modern twist and a five-storey Georgian townhouse(n)

programme.title: Lorraine's Fast, Fresh and Easy Food
programme.desc: Lorraine Pascale demonstrates how to make more quick and easy meals from scratch, turning her attention to dishes that satisfy cravings and lighten the mood. Among her creations are meals designed for a quiet night in with friends, as well as food for people eating alone(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: Sharon has a breakthrough confirming all her suspicions about Rory. Following a heavy night in the pub with his workmates, Paudge wakes up in a strange bed with no idea where he is(n)

programme.title: The Great British Winter
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison visits Britain's largest estuarine habitat in Morecambe Bay to discover the best-kept secrets of the winter landscape, including an unusual gentlemen's club of grey seals on Walney Island. She also reveals why both birds and fishermen are attracted to the area during the harsh weather(n)

programme.title: Big Blue UK
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and his team focus on the array of weird and wonderful creatures on the UK's seashore, and Richard Taylor Jones goes in search of the barrel jellyfish(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Claire Rawle and James Lewis are at St Albans Cathedral, where items to be valued include a whalebone walking stick and an autograph book with signatures of the Rolling Stones(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Samira Ahmed, Dom Joly, Angela Rippon and John Robins test their general knowledge skills ahead of Friday's all-important final(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo journeys from Maltby to Hinckley, going whippet racing and trying to understand the appeal of Marmite, before a look around the Triumph motorcycle factory(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Bananarama's Sara Dallin and Keren Woodward go hunting for antiques in Buckinghamshire, joined by experts Charles Hanson and David Harper. Before an auction, Keren goes back in time to extol the dance routines of the Georgian period, and Sara and David to learn how Black Beauty inspired a Victorian horse sanctuary that is still operating to this day(n)

programme.title: Secrets of the Museum
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at the V&A, curator Keith is trying to breathe life into an original Stormtrooper costume from The Empire Strikes Back. However, the helmet is missing. In the Rock and Pop archive, curator Vicky is examining a photograph donated after the V&A's David Bowie Exhibition. And a road sign, made in 1961 by graphic designer Margaret Calvert, is also added to the museum's collection(n)

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: Eighty-one-year-old Joe has been left with a hole in his heart after suffering a heart attack, and face a complex procedure that carries a high-mortality rate. Ellie, 28, is one of the growing population of adults with congenital heart disease, who had devices fitted into their hearts as children, and need further procedures as they get older. Already cancelled twice before, she hopes that this time her operation to replace her pulmonary valve with keyhole surgery will go ahead, so she can begin to plan her wedding to Jade(n)

programme.title: Vic & Bob's Big Night Out
programme.desc: Judge Nutmeg dispenses punishment to an unsuspecting audience member, the Ghost Hunters are on the prowl, and Bob takes viewers back to the 1980s with a special performance. Comedy, written by and starring Vic Reeves and Bob Mortimer(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emma Barnett(n)

programme.title: Win the Wilderness: Alaska
programme.desc: Duane and Rena question each couple about their plans for the home to see if any of them are harbouring doubts about their ability to make a success of life in the wilderness. This round of questioning results in one couple being eliminated from the contest, while the remaining three are separated from each other for two days to test their ability to cope with isolation(n)

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: Britain's departure from the EU brings changes for Newlyn, Cornwall's biggest fishing port. The harbourmaster oversees a redevelopment of the fish market, as well as making plans to transform the harbour's ageing infrastructure. However, many fishermen resent his grand plans, feeling that they were not consulted over massive changes to their working environment. While many fishermen supported leaving the EU to gain control over quotas, many now fear that their industry will be sacrificed during negotiations(n)

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood samples life working as a game warden in Cameron County, Texas, where he joins warden Mike Gonzalez to learn about the challenges he faces in this role(n)

programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: Michael Mosley tests whether an extra hour a week in nature improves your health, and lain Gregoire investigates an illness that can transform a child's personality overnight. Meanwhile, Giles Yeo investigates whether trendy plant-based milks are any healthier than dairy, and Guddi Singh witnesses pioneering cancer surgery(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Neil Oliver explores Worm's Head on the Gower Peninsula, and learns about the quarrying heritage of Abereiddy and Porthgain, while Alice Roberts discovers the musical properties of the beach on Porth Oer(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor helps a retired couple escape to the tranquillity of Norfolk after 30 years in a busy Essex town. Now that their children have flown the nest, the pair are hoping to find a country home that's full of character for their £350,000 budget. Elsewhere, Margherita discovers Norfolk's answer to the Great Barrier Reef(n)

programme.title: Winterwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham, Michaela Strachan, Gillian Burke and Iolo Williams present more wildlife reports live from their base in the Cairngorms. They reveal the science behind why ducks don't die of cold, hear about the mini beasts captured on camera by a passionate amateur photographer and share the mass spectacle of 30,000 redwings roosting in a remote wood in Lancashire's Ribble Valley(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and her guests discuss the big political issues of the day. Plus, updates from Westminster and beyond(n)

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: In light of Britain's baking obsession, actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of recipes from television chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: In York, the winners of the best detached, best apartment, best semi-detached and best wildcard property are revisited by the judges for a second inspection. The arbiters must make their ultimate decision and crown only one of them the week's Best House in Town(n)

programme.title: Lorraine's Fast, Fresh and Easy Food
programme.desc: Lorraine Pascale prepares more dishes designed to show that cooking quality food need not be a time-consuming and difficult process. In this episode, she turns her attention to her favourite classic dishes, and demonstrates how to make them both more appetising and easier to prepare(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: A distraught Aoife Burke arrives at the station, furious following Ash and Paudge's failure to inform her of the discovery of her mother's body(n)

programme.title: The Great British Winter
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison concludes her exploration of Britain's winter landscapes with a trip to the Shetland archipelago. Here she witnesses the mesmerising beauty of the Northern Lights, encounters Viking practices alongside present-day crofting and learns about seal and otter pup rescues - each of which help prove life is far from dull for the islands' inhabitants during this bleak season(n)

programme.title: Big Blue UK
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall heads to the island of Lundy to discover why nearly half the world's population of grey seals choose to live in UK waters. Meanwhile, Lindsey Chapman helps return a stranded seal pup to the sea and wildlife cameraman Richard Taylor-Jones tracks down a group of the common seals living in a surprisingly urban setting. Plus, Matt Baker witnesses an extraordinary gathering of animals in Monterey Bay, America(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Blackburn Cathedral, Charles Hanson values a rare Eureka clock and Anita Manning is drawn to four oil paintings of the Ribble Valley. Host Paul Martin meets up with Michelin-star chef Nigel Haworth to discover how to make the perfect Lancashire hotpot(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Samira Ahmed, Dom Joly, Angela Rippon and John Robins test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games, with the week's overall champion set to be crowned(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Armed with his Bradshaw's guide, Michael Portillo reaches Birmingham and discovers how a radical reformer made his mark on the 'city of a thousand trades'. At Highbury Hall, he finds Joseph Chamberlain's mansion home and hears how he established a political dynasty including a prime minister and a foreign secretary. Next stop is Cradley Heath, once one of five chain-making towns in the West Midlands. At the centre of Britain's ceramic industry in Stoke-on-Trent, Michael visits Wedgwood, established by 'Queen's Potter' Josiah Wedgwood in 1759 and visited in 1913 by King George V and Queen Mary(n)

programme.title: Extreme Wales with Richard Parks
programme.desc: The endurance athlete embarks on three adrenaline-filled journeys that explore the very best of Wales and what it has to offer for the keen adventurer. He begins with a four-day cycle ride from the Brecon Beacons to the summit of Snowdon, meeting a mountain-biking pioneer and receiving a lesson from the world downhill champion along the way(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Comedians Jamali Maddix and Bobby Mair compete in West Yorkshire, guided by experts Natasha Raskin Sharp and Angus Ashworth. Between browsing for bargains and battling for the best punchline, Angus and Bobby explore the fascinating history of the printed page around the city of Leeds, while Jamali and Natasha explore how the strange evolution of the optical toy led to the profusion of games and media encountered today. In spite of advice, one funnyman thinks a porcelain parrot will be profitable, while the other invests in a toy robot. The duo then take their purchases to auction in Bourne End, Buckinghamshire(n)

programme.title: Our Coast
programme.desc: Adrian Chiles and Mehreen Baig explore the south-western coastline of Scotland, with Mehreen taking to the skies with former RAF fighter pilot Archie Liggat, who is now using his skills to document large-scale pollution hidden along the coastline. Adrian, meanwhiile, visits Portpatrick to meet Calum Currie, who has persuaded his community to buy shares in the town's harbour - revitalising it for all who live there. Plus, Patrick Aryee joins volunteers help to track rare birdlife on the Ailsa Craig and Joe Lindsay visits an artists' colony that was the setting for cult film The Wicker Man(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: The last of two compilations of highlights from the 17th series of the quiz with a difference, hosted by Sandi Toksvig. Guests including Tom Allen, Jo Brand, Susan Calman, Alan Carr, Jimmy Carr, Victoria Coren Mitchell, Phill Jupitus, Nish Kumar, Prue Leith, Joe Lycett, Jason Manford, Sarah Millican, David Mitchell, Johnny Vegas, Holly Walsh and Josh Widdicombe joined regular panellist Alan Davies to answer questions based on subjects beginning with the letter Q(n)

programme.title: Frankie Boyle's Tour of Scotland
programme.desc: The comedian explores Scottish creativity on a journey from Oban to Glasgow. Travelling through the Highlands, Frankie visits the home of Grant Morrison, who has written stories for some of the comic world's most iconic characters. Resuming his trek, he travels from Dunoon to Bannockburn, the site of Robert the Bruce's most famous battle, where he learns how to fight with a sword and shield just like the Scots Knights of the 1300s. Frankie also witnesses young artists brightening up the darker corners of Glasgow and reflects on the city's shameful past, profiting off slavery in the British colonies(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's important national and international news stories, with Katie Razzall(n)

programme.title: The Big Sick (2017)
programme.desc: A struggling stand-up comedian proves a hit with a student heckler, but their affair ends just before a mystery illness puts the object of his affections in a coma. Finding himself absorbed with Emily's care and bonding with her sparring parents, Kumail also has to struggle with his own Muslim relatives and their expectations. Romantic comedy, starring Kumail Nanjiani, Zoe Kazan, Holly Hunter and Ray Romano(n)

programme.title: Live Cycling
programme.desc: Coverage from the Track Cycling World Championships at the Velodrom in Berlin, featuring the men's and women's team sprint finals and the women's 10km scratch race. Great Britain's Elinor Barker won the scratch event last year and is in the squad once again(n)

programme.title: A Very British History
programme.desc: Dr Aminul Hoque tells the story of the thousands of Bangladeshi families who settled in the UK in the 1970s and '80s, bringing new foods, traditions and workers for British industry. Using film archive and his own experiences of growing up in east London, Aminul meets and tells the stories of Bengalis who made their homes here over the course of two key decades, and returns to Bangladesh with his own family, taking his children for their first visit to their grandparent's village to explore their roots in the region of Sylhet(n)

programme.title: Queen Elizabeth's Secret Agents
programme.desc: The docudrama focuses on the machinations of ambitious aristocrat the Earl of Essex as he tries to take over Robert Cecil's spy network in an attempt to gain control over the ageing Elizabeth I and hold the power to choose the next king of England(n)

programme.title: The Making of Spain
programme.desc: Simon Sebag Montefiore explores the history of Spain, beginning with the country's early years, when Iberia was a minor province of Carthage - then the most coveted of Rome's colonies - through to Spain's Muslim age and the Cordoba Caliphate(n)

programme.title: Treasures of Ancient Greece
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke explores the riches and unique legacy of Greek art, telling the story of its mysterious origins as he starts his journey at the palace of Knossos in Crete. He then travels to Santorini and finds gold in the fabled stronghold of Mycenae, and uncovers the beginnings of a defining spirit in Greek art - embracing mythology, a passion for symmetry, and an obsession with the human body(n)

programme.title: Victorian Sensations
programme.desc: In the final episode, psychotherapist Philippa Perry explores ghosts, science and early cinema in the 1890s, and how the late Victorian passion for science co-exists with a deeply-held belief in the paranormal(n)

programme.title: Bauhaus Rules with Vic Reeves
programme.desc: Six graduates from Central St Martins are challenged to create a new work of art, design or performance inspired by artists who taught at the Bauhaus. Key figures from today's worlds of art and design - who have been influenced by the Bauhaus in their own work - set the rules for the experiment. Presented by Vic Reeves(n)

programme.title: A Very British History
programme.desc: Dr Aminul Hoque tells the story of the thousands of Bangladeshi families who settled in the UK in the 1970s and '80s, bringing new foods, traditions and workers for British industry. Using film archive and his own experiences of growing up in east London, Aminul meets and tells the stories of Bengalis who made their homes here over the course of two key decades, and returns to Bangladesh with his own family, taking his children for their first visit to their grandparent's village to explore their roots in the region of Sylhet(n)

programme.title: Live Cycling
programme.desc: Coverage from the Track Cycling World Championships at the Velodrom in Berlin, featuring the men's and women's team pursuit finals and the men's keirin final. Australia beat Great Britain into second place in both team pursuits last year in Poland, while Matthijs Buchli of the Netherlands prevailed in the keirin(n)

programme.title: Handmade
programme.desc: Filmed in real time and without voiceover or music, this series examines the crafting of handmade objects. The first programme observes expert Michael Ruh carrying out the time-consuming process of blowing and rolling glass to make a simple jug. Glass is heated in a steel crucible until it becomes liquid. Ruh's task is to keep a globule hot and in constant motion as he shapes it(n)

programme.title: Pompeii: New Secrets Revealed with Mary Beard
programme.desc: The renowned classicist and TV historian explores what life was like 2,000 years ago in Pompeii, one of the world's most extraordinary and iconic archaeological sites. She goes behind the scenes of the Great Pompeii Project, where restoration teams have gradually removed the layers of time and deterioration from the frescoes and mosaics of houses closed to the public for decades, and with the help of point-cloud scanning technology, Pompeii is seen and explained like never before(n)

programme.title: Queen Elizabeth's Secret Agents
programme.desc: Elizabeth is dead and James VI of Scotland travels south to take the throne at the invitation of Robert Cecil, but the new king has his own ideas and has little time for the late queen's spymaster. Soon Cecil's problems worsen when learns that his arch enemy John Gerard has escaped detention and made contact with a splinter cell in the Catholic underground with a plan to blow-up Parliament. Cecil must negotiate between warring factions to bring down what would become known as the Gunpowder Plot(n)

programme.title: How We Built Britain
programme.desc: David Dimbleby travels around the north of England to learn about the industrial hub of Queen Victoria's Britain, where progress affected people in radically different ways. He travels by steam to Newcastle before venturing south-west to Manchester, discovering vast warehouses based on Venetian palaces and a town hall with distinctly Gothic architecture. His journey also takes him to the grand merchant houses, music halls and shopping arcades of Leeds(n)

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Liz Bonnin marks the 50th anniversary of the construction of the Severn Bridge in a Timeshift special telling the inside story of its design and construction. As the motorway link between England and Wales, the Severn Bridge was described as 'the most perfect suspension bridge in the world', and Liz reveals how its unique, slim-line structure came about by accident(n)

programme.title: The Silk Road
programme.desc: Sam Willis continues his journey in Iran, the first time a BBC crew has been allowed into the country for nearly 10 years. He visits Persepolis, the city that was at the heart of the Persian empire, before following an ancient caravan route through the desert. Along the way, he stops at a Zoroastrian temple before arriving at Istanbul, where he discovers the influence the silk trade had on the Roman empire, and finally returns to Venice, where he visits Marco Polo's house(n)

programme.title: Pappano's Greatest Arias
programme.desc: Antonio Pappano, conductor and music director of the Royal Opera House, shares his favourite arias from the medium's 400-year history, taking into account such varied traditions as baroque, romantic, verismo and bel canto, as well as more contemporary techniques. The programme also includes workshops with some of the biggest opera stars on the modern stage, including Joyce DiDonato, Lucy Crowe, Bryan Hymel and Lawrence Brownlee, as well as classic archive performances from Placido Domingo, Gundula Janowitz and Piero Cappuccilli(n)

programme.title: Pompeii: New Secrets Revealed with Mary Beard
programme.desc: The renowned classicist and TV historian explores what life was like 2,000 years ago in Pompeii, one of the world's most extraordinary and iconic archaeological sites. She goes behind the scenes of the Great Pompeii Project, where restoration teams have gradually removed the layers of time and deterioration from the frescoes and mosaics of houses closed to the public for decades, and with the help of point-cloud scanning technology, Pompeii is seen and explained like never before(n)

programme.title: Live Cycling
programme.desc: The Track Cycling World Championships continue at the Velodrom in Berlin, where medals are decided in five events - the men's points race, time-trial and individual pursuit, and the women's sprint and omnium. Perhaps surprisingly given their impressive results in recent years, Great Britain were unable to add to the one gold they had won at this stage last year in Poland when these disciplines also took place, with the Netherlands topping the medal table just ahead of Australia(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Mark Goodier hosts the edition first shown on April 13, featuring performances by Cookie Crew with Edwin Starr, Ten City, T'Pau, INXS, Fine Young Cannibals, U2 and BB King, Paul Simpson with Adeva, the Bangles and Yello(n)

programme.title: Eric Burdon: Rock 'n' Roll - Animal
programme.desc: How pop group the Animals were important standard bearers of the 'British Invasion' of America, behind the Beatles but ahead of the Rolling Stones, the Who and the Kinks. Alongside his passion for original American blues, frontman Eric Burdon got together in the late 1960s with the black LA band WAR - itself a political statement in the Black Panther era - and, inspired by Charlie Parker, John Coltrane and Rahsaan Roland Kirk, expanded his musical spectrum with jazz and funk. This documentary reveals how Burdon's creative output has made an important and profoundly authentic contribution to popular culture(n)

programme.title: Guitar, Drum and Bass
programme.desc: The former Police musician explores the drums as the founding instrument of popular modern music. Beats that travelled from Africa, via New Orleans and across the world, are the consistent force behind musical evolution. Stewart plays with some of the most inspiring drummers of the past 50 years, including John Densmore of the Doors, Chad Smith of Red Hot Chili Peppers, Prince's musical director Sheila E, New Order's Stephen Morris and the Foo Fighters' Taylor Hawkins. He also goes dancing in New Orleans, builds his own bass drum pedal and checks out hot new bands on Santa Monica beach(n)

programme.title: Burt Bacharach: A Life in Song
programme.desc: Coverage of a unique concert staged at London's Royal Festival Hall celebrating the music of songwriter and performer Burt Bacharach. Some of his most famous songs are performed by artists including Alfie Boe, Sophie Ellis-Bextor, Shaun Escoffery, Rebecca Ferguson, Justin Hayward, Michael Kiwanuka, Laura Mvula and Joss Stone. Burt himself also performs, accompanied by Josie James and his band, and chats to Michael Grade about the art of songwriting and shares the stories behind some of his best-loved hits(n)

programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Lucy Alexander follow the progress of properties in the London borough of Camden, Coatbridge in North Lanarkshire and Bootle on Merseyside(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with Viv and Darren Stirk, who five years earlier spent a trial week in New Zealand, considering whether to move there with their three children(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers investigate an explosion that left two brothers dead, while the victim of a motor insurance scam is determined not to let the fraudsters get away with it(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning presents from Kingston upon Thames, south-west London, where the red and blue teams explore an antique centre in the company of experts Catherine Southon and Danny Sebastian. The auction of their gatherings is later held in nearby Chiswick(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Valerie has to deal with a tricky teenage patient who starts acting up once away from her mother. Meanwhile, Emma and Jasmine go on a breakfast date(n)

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: When an amnesiac wanders into their lives, Frank Hathaway and Luella Shakespeare must work out what happened to him. Comedy drama, starring Mark Benton and Jo Joyner(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A retired Burton upon Trent couple look for a south Devon home, with help from Alistair Appleton. He tries to find them the best property for their £540,000 budget(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Families of shire horse breeders exhibit their prize winning pedigrees and compete for glory and the top prizes at the Royal Cheshire Show(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and Paul Laidlaw continue their search for antiques in the Black Country, with Paul finding a piggy bank, and Catherine opting for something distinctly oriental(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present the magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Max finds herself in a desolate place after receiving some distressing news, and following her rift with Ric, she may have lost her closest confidante. Meanwhile, Jac and Kian must decide who will be Darwin's next clinical lead, and shocking revelations threaten Essie and Dom's friendship. Starring Hugh Quarshie and Marcus Griffiths(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The next six amateurs try to prove themselves and impress judges John Torode and Gregg Wallace, presenting them with a dish of their own design. John and Gregg will pick their two favourites, and those cooks earn themselves a place in the next round. The four remaining contestants are now challenged to cook off in a new test - The Three Ingredients Challenge - conjuring up a dish using squid, courgette and chilli(n)

programme.title: Nolan Live
programme.desc: Stephen Nolan probes the issues that matter to the people of Northern Ireland, focusing on the stories of the week and the people making the headlines in lively debates with the studio audience and guest panellists(n)

programme.title: Eating with My Ex
programme.desc: Celeb-obsessed Navid is wondering what went wrong with model Charlotte, but she is confused as to why he has invited her to a steak restaurant when she is a vegetarian. Sam wants to find out if Chesca is the one that got away - but she might be enjoying the single life too much to look back. Mark is keen to learn if Justin and he have a future together, but the two drag queens might not make it through dinner without losing their cool(n)

programme.title: Killing Eve
programme.desc: Desk-bound MI5 security officer Eve Polastri is bored in her job and yearns for a more exciting life. When a Russian politician is murdered, she is given the task of protecting the only witness and soon finds herself on a collision course with a psychopathic assassin known as Villanelle. First episode of the spy thriller series, based on Luke Jennings' Codename Villanelle novella series and adapted for television by Phoebe Waller-Bridge (Fleabag). Starring Jodie Comer and Sandra Oh(n)

programme.title: Killing Eve
programme.desc: In the aftermath of her recent assignment, Villanelle is ordered to take a break, but she goes ahead with her next mission regardless. Meanwhile, after being fired by Frank for the debacle at the hospital, Eve is stunned to be presented with her dream opportunity - a secret, off-the-books department, a mission she is truly invested in, and the chance to build and lead her own team. Her target is Villanelle. Spy thriller, adapted for television by Phoebe Waller-Bridge and starring Jodie Comer and Sandra Oh(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Sally Nugent(n)

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal
programme.desc: Matt Allwright investigates the scandal of a rented terrace house that is overcrowded and riddled with cockroaches, and meets a Midlands family fighting vermin in their property(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore properties about to be auctioned in Bilston, Wolverhampton, Bignall End in Staffordshire and Brimrod in Rochdale(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with Tracey and Paul Nicholls and their three children to discover whether they decided to make the move to Auckland and live there permanently(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: The Defenders crack down on dealers selling dangerous body building drugs online and the travel agents conning families out of thousands of pounds(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles is joined by Caroline Hawley and Raj Bisram at the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells and learns all about a traditional Welsh love token(n)

programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Emma goes to Zara for some medical advice, while Karen and Rob prepare to celebrate with Abz. Ruhma gets involved with a new mother struggling to bond with her child(n)

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: Frank and Lu investigate a death threat at Runningbrook, an exclusive tennis club that hides a toxic threat. Crime drama, starring Mark Benton and Jo Joyner(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin goes house hunting in the Northumberland countryside with a couple wanting to escape busy urban life in Gateshead and who have a £500,000 budget to move three generations of the family into a new rural home. Along the way, Jonnie visits Bamburgh Castle, home to the ancient kings of Northumbria. While taking in some of its illustrious history, he lends a helping hand with the year-round conservation tasks necessary to maintain one of the county's most iconic heritage buildings(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two pedigree cattle farmers battle it out with the best of the best at the Royal Welsh Show, Europe's largest agricultural event, where winning means everything to their families(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and Paul Laidlaw visit Birmingham, where one grisly find features a gallows and a break from the shops uncovers the story of the real Peaky Blinders(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest, with Matt Baker and Alex Jones(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: It's the day of Jean's oncology appointment and Daniel forces her to go. At the appointment, she is overwhelmed to hear that her MRI scan has come back clear. After returning home, Jean lies to Daniel saying she doesn't have the result, so Mo breaks the good news(n)

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Journalist Nikki Fox teams up with scientists at Liverpool John Moores University to discover what takeaways do to our health, what's really in them and how people can kick the habit. In a unique experiment, 15 volunteers eat nothing but takeaways for two weeks and undergo a barrage of tests, from blood sugar and body fat to gut bacteria and mental sharpness. The results reveal the shocking truth about how takeaways affect our bodies and brains(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A blind actress is the only witness to her husband's murder, but it remains to be seen whether she can really be trusted. Drama, starring Ralf Little, Aude Legastelois and Frances Tomelty(n)

programme.title: The View
programme.desc: A review of the week's political news, including reports on the latest events at Stormont and Westminster. Plus, comment and analysis with studio guests(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce hosts the political debate from Middlesbrough, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Breakfast

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal
programme.desc: Liverpool Council's housing team works on the case of a family whose members left their rented property in an appalling state - and the landlord in shock(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore properties about to be auctioned in Wednesfield, Wolverhampton, Sydenham in south London and Chatham in Kent, then talk to the new owners(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: South African-born Candice Crawford hopes to persuade husband Sean to move to New Zealand permanently after sampling a week in Tauranga. Presented by Nicki Chapman(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Officers search the home of a loan shark and find a safe containing thousands of pounds. The safe proves to be a breakthrough in their investigation into an illegal money lending operation in Dudley in the West Midlands. The loan shark had made hundreds of thousands of pounds by pushing hard-up families further into debt. The enforcers also force a rogue builder to pay compensation to his elderly victims(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Caroline Hawley and Raj Bisram assist the red and blue teams as they look for items of interest and value in Detling, Kent. Presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp monitors proceeding and also visits a nearby farm to lend a hand with the hop harvest(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al looks forward to the night of the lock-in at the Icon, though as soon as he arrives, his expectations are dampened. Jimmi gets a new cellmate who challenges him to a game of cards, with the winner getting to ask a question of the loser(n)

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: Tracking a local conman, Frank and Lu make some unlikely allies when they encounter a pair of deadly gangsters. Mark Benton and Jo Joyner star in the comedy drama about an oddball couple of private detectives who investigate crime in Stratford-upon-Avon(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a couple up sticks from Birmingham and spend £500,000 on a country home that allows them to lead the good life while being close enough to Hereford for regular visits to their daughter. While in Herefordshire, Jules visits a family-run blacksmiths that forges commissioned pieces for historic buildings, and takes to the anvil to make a wrought iron accessory for his own home(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Sometimes taking big risks can make a difference to the farming industry, and here three inspirational farming families are celebrated for their ideas and ingenuity. However, only one can be crowned Diversification Farmer of the Year at the Farmers Weekly Awards. Narrated by Bill Paterson(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and Paul Laidlaw peruse antique shops in Oxfordshire, Gloucestershire, Warwickshire and Northamptonshire as they head to the final auction in Middle Littleton, Worcestershire. Plus, a visit to Shakespeare's birthplace in Stratford-upon-Avon to find out about a 19th-century, Bard-inspired, American black actor(n)

programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey host the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Dave and Harry Rich are in Hereford, where they come up with ideas to transform a back garden for a family that has four generations living in the same house. With a limited budget, the designers come up with some ideas for creating garden sculptures and focal points on a shoestring(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: FIFA Women's World Cup Golden Boot winner Ellen White, Great Britain 400m runner Perri Shakes-Drayton, former Everton midfielder Leon Osman and Grand National-winning jockey Mick Fitzgerald join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: As everyone tries to get back to normal following recent events, one person's lies begin to unravel. Meanwhile, Karen inspires Keegan with an idea to cheer up Tiffany, but Keegan's plan ends in disaster(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The first quarter-final sees the six talented heat winners return to fight for a place in Knockout Week. Facing the Critic's Test, the week's best-performing amateurs take on an intimidating task, cooking to a brief set by top food critic Jay Rayner, who asks them to produce an exceptional dish incorporating stuffing. Hosted by John Torode and Gregg Wallace and narrated by India Fisher(n)

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Newlyweds Dermot and Maria argue over whether they should buy their own home. Meanwhile, Grandad hopes to witness his own funeral, but Mrs Brown's plans go awry when she mistakes two Mormons - who have been stealing Father Quinn's parishioners - for the men who are supposed to be delivering the coffin(n)

programme.title: Soft Border Patrol
programme.desc: With Brexit over the line Lisa McCoy meets with her political neighbours, just as an Irish politician arrives from Dublin. Niall Sweeney's new in-car technology throws up a few surprises and football legend Jackie Fullerton has some advice for the patrollers. With Karen Hassan, Neil Delamere, Michael Condron, Chris Patrick-Simpson and guest star Jackie Fullerton(n)

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: Strictly Come Dancing's AJ Pritchard and his brother Curtis choreograph the three queens as they take on an all-singing, all-dancing final challenge. programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate when ladies' man and rowing club member Guy Sweetman is found dead in the river during the annual Midsomer Regatta. They soon discover the elite exterior of the club hides a world of money problems and sexual jealousy and the victim had a number of enemies who might have wanted him dead. Guest starring Olympic rower Steve Redgrave and Diana Quick(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Paul Nicholas guest stars as a Swedish sailor who waits for his ship home in Bernie's funeral parlour - but may not be telling the truth about the contents of the coffin he's escorting. A trio of young joyriders steal Carol's car while she is attending to a patient and decide to experiment with the drugs they find in her medical bag as well as selling them on to interested parties. Rosie and Fergus struggle to get their fledgling relationship off the ground as a series of interruptions plague their dates, while Bellamy starts to wonder whether trying again with Gina is really such a good idea. With Lisa Kay, Vanessa Hehir, Mark Jordon and Tricia Penrose(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth takes a break on the island of Rhodes, where an English beauty draws attention by flirting with fellow guests at the Palace Hotel. When she is found murdered, her husband is immediately suspected, but Poirot quickly deduces there is more to the case than a simple crime of passion. Whodunit, starring David Suchet(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A professor feels humiliated when her internet dating video is leaked onto a website and watched by her students. The next morning she is found dead, the victim of what appears to be a straightforward suicide. However, Lewis is not convinced and digs deeper, identifying several suspects including the site's founder - but as he and Hathaway get closer to the truth amid a rising death toll, they find their personal and professional lives dredged up online for all to see. Guest starring Roxanne McKee (Game of Thrones) and Toby Stephens (Die Another Day), with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Three weeks after reporting her husband missing, adult-film star Vicky Birkinshaw is arrested on suspicion of his murder. Rachel faces the task of going through the suspect's home-made pornography collection in search of clues, but the case takes a dark twist when Vicky's daughter turns up with her much older boyfriend. Meanwhile, Janet's affair with a colleague is exposed. Detective drama, starring Lesley Sharp and Suranne Jones(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: An opera singer is shot by a sniper at an Oxford University ceremony just as she is about to receive an honorary degree. Morse is a big fan of her work and thinks she could not possibly be involved in anything untoward, but his investigations turn up more than a few sordid secrets. Detective drama, starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Robert Hardy, Sheila Gish, John Gielgud and Rachel Weisz(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A man accused of murdering five people is killed in prison shortly before going to trial, wrapping up a lot of loose ends and saving the state the expense of keeping him behind bars indefinitely. Alas, Morse cannot help wondering why the body of one of his victims was never found - and the chance discovery of a corpse gives him the perfect opportunity to investigate further. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star(n)

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Dr Price and his wife hire a housekeeper, little realising that the woman is his long-lost mother(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A man accused of murdering five people is killed in prison shortly before going to trial, wrapping up a lot of loose ends and saving the state the expense of keeping him behind bars indefinitely. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: When a guest at the Aidensfield Arms explains she's searching for her missing son, Blaketon offers to help - but as his hunt progresses, it becomes clear somebody is trying to do him harm. Peggy turns down David's offer of dinner on her birthday in favour of a date with an old flame, and Bellamy finally opens up about his troubles under pressure from Walker(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim tells Frank that she does not want to go away on honeymoon. Angharad asks Bernard to consider Jessica's feelings about Biff. Nick is nervous about his blind date(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Linda tells Zoe she is pleased to have her job back. Luke informs Angharad and Bernard that he is not leaving Emmerdale. Seth's pride gets in the way of reconciling with Betty(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre refuses to believe Samir was violent at the airport and worries about him. Alma urges Ken to get on with his life without Denise(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Mike tells Deirdre to fight for Samir and offers her money to help. Raquel is touched when Sarah Louise asks to be a bridesmaid. Maud feels guilty about letting Maureen cope alone(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott enter the world of botany to search for the killer of a retired teacher found dead shortly after smuggling a rare orchid out of Borneo. The discovery of two more bodies makes the detectives realise the murderer will stop at nothing to get hold of the coveted plant, and it seems the key to the mystery may lie in a Latin notebook. John Nettles and John Hopkins star(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: When a guest at the Aidensfield Arms explains she's searching for her missing son, Blaketon offers to help - but as his hunt progresses, it becomes clear somebody is trying to do him harm. Peggy turns down David's offer of dinner on her birthday in favour of a date with an old flame, and Bellamy finally opens up about his troubles under pressure from Walker(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective's curiosity is aroused by the passengers on board a luxury cruise ship bound for Egypt - particularly a woman who appears inordinately offensive to her fellow travellers. When the vessel arrives at Alexandria, the irritating lady's body is discovered in her cabin - leaving the sleuth with a host of possible suspects to interrogate. David Suchet stars(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: It is the annual Portwenn versus Port Carran gig race and school gig festival, and this time Penhale is the umpire and Morwenna is the Portwenn team captain. However, Morwenna is forced to find a replacement rower when Eric dislocates his finger. Meanwhile, Mel Hendy goes to see Martin at the surgery worried about a rash that could be infectious, but she ends up opening up to him about her concerns over her husband's drinking. Elsewhere, Martin and Louisa arrive home to find Chris Parsons waiting for them(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Martin prepares to face his hearing, but despite having closed the practice, he finds all his patients are trying to make appointments. An American tourist who visited Portwenn previously returns to the town, hoping to research her family tree. However, she also has a medical issue, and comes to the troubled doctor seeking his advice. Louisa goes to see Chris to find out how serious the situation is, while the townsfolk take steps to show Martin how much he is appreciated. Sigourney Weaver reprises her guest role from the previous series(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Janet moves a step closer to tracking down Veronica's killer while investigating a recent murder that bears similarities to the cold case. Rachel finds out about an affair Nick once had with a juror in the middle of a trial - a revelation that could end his career. Detective drama, starring Lesley Sharp, Suranne Jones and Rupert Graves(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: English country squire Marcus Waverly receives a series of anonymous letters threatening to kidnap his son Johnnie, but the case is dismissed by Scotland Yard - prompting Poirot to open his own investigation. Drama, starring David Suchet and Geoffrey Bateman(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Denise refuses to speak to Ken, and Fiona is shocked when Steve asks her to move in with him. Audrey threatens to resign if Alf does not get the limo back(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Gail is happy when Nick says he will spend Christmas at home. Fiona worries about losing her job. Reg tells Maureen he wants them to have a life away from the shop(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Ned tells Linda to start pulling her weight around the house. Frank wants to set up a trust fund for Chris and Rachel's baby. Betty's caravan goes for an unexpected journey(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Angharad tries to talk to Bernard about their future. Caroline nags Nick about getting a girlfriend. Eric calls into the Woolpack and demands his money back from Alan(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Chaos reigns after an unknown sniper shoots a woman as she leaves a meeting of the flower show committee. DS Rachel Dawson's enquiries reveal the gunman may have missed his intended target - and when Blaketon spots an article in the local paper, he realises the bullet might have been aimed at him. Meanwhile, Peggy buys an ice-cream van to take advantage of the hot weather, but makes the mistake of leaving David in charge of the project. Lindsey Coulson guests alongside Derek Fowlds and Clare Wille(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part one of two. Primrose falls for a roofing contractor, while the twins prove too much of a handful for their school, prompting a trip to view the facilities on offer at Brockhurst College. Nostalgic comedy drama, starring David Jason and Pam Ferris(n)

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Angie persuades Robert to arrange an evening out with the lads _ but neither she nor Ida are prepared for the consequences(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: An unusual message appears in Chipping Cleghorn's local newspaper, revealing details of a forthcoming murder and, while at first glance it looks like a practical joke, the information proves to be deadly accurate. Miss Marple helps Inspector Craddock with his investigation and the pair soon uncover several likely suspects in the village. Feature-length drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Zoe Wanamaker, Elaine Paige, Virginia McKenna, Cherie Lunghi and Frances Barber(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The Aidensfield policemen are called to subdue anti-war protestors at a disused airfield, but the event has serious repercussions for Walker after he accidentally hits one of the mob with his truncheon. Younger is gutted to discover Rosie has feelings for new vet Fergus, who is the indirect cause of chaos when a cobra escapes from his car at the garage. Irritated that nobody's taking her snake-catching suggestions seriously, Peggy refuses to help with the hunt and drives off with David - straight into the arms of danger. With Jonathan Kerrigan, Gwen Taylor, Vanessa Hehir and Andrew Langtree(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Betty is worried that she might go to prison for hitting a policeman. Seth spies on the Dingles and notices their blue van. Tina causes a scene at the parents' evening(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Alan threatens to call the police to investigate Eric's business affairs. Dave tries to kiss Kathy, and Bernard suggests to Angharad that they move away from Emmerdale(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Betty is thrilled and proud to wear the Mayoral chain, Denise asks Jon to buy the salon, and Raquel is stunned when Curly asks her to marry him(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Curly starts work at Soopascoopa and is not impressed by the uniform. Immigration officers turn up and want to speak to Samir about working illegally(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The naked body of a business executive is found dumped in a rival factory's steriliser machine, leaving Barnaby and Scott with a complicated mystery to unravel. Their investigation begins with factory owners the Plummers, who seem to be hiding more than their share of secrets. Detective drama, starring John Nettles, John Hopkins, Annette Crosbie and Jasper Britton(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The Aidensfield policemen are called to subdue anti-war protestors at a disused airfield, but the event has serious repercussions for Walker after he accidentally hits one of the mob with his truncheon. Irritated that nobody's taking her snake-catching suggestions seriously, Peggy refuses to help with the hunt and drives off with David - straight into the arms of danger. With Jonathan Kerrigan, Gwen Taylor, Vanessa Hehir and Andrew Langtree(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: After enjoying a trip to the theatre with Captain Hastings, the detective is disturbed by the sound of gunshots in an apartment at Whitehaven Mansions, and soon finds himself investigating a murder. Drama, starring David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: In Dover, Lieutenant Colin Race is investigating the deaths of two Navy personnel when a distraught Sheila Webb runs out of 19 Wilbraham Crescent and into his arms. Inside the house, Colin finds the body of a murder victim surrounded by stopped clocks. Mystery, starring David Suchet, Anna Massey, Jaime Winstone, Lesley Sharp, Phil Daniels, Geoffrey Palmer and Tessa Peake-Jones(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Rachel discovers her ex-partner is the defending barrister for Georgios Stelikos, a man on trial for rape and murder, and she is horrified when the accused escapes conviction. When Stelikos is later found dead, the prime suspect is Hannah Conway, who made public death threats against him after giving evidence for the prosecution in court. Meanwhile, Janet tries to support her colleague following devastating news. Detective drama, starring Lesley Sharp and Suranne Jones(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A homicidal rapist escapes from prison and embarks on a deadly game of cat and mouse with Morse, relying on his mastery of disguise and bizarre occult connections to stay one step ahead of the law. Only the madman's psychiatrist seems able to provide a lead as to his whereabouts - and her life is in mortal danger as a result. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately, Keith Allen, Richard Griffiths and Harriet Walter(n)

programme.title: Tony Robinson: Coast to Coast
programme.desc: In North Yorkshire, the presenter discovers how the longest stretch of steam railway would have disappeared, but for a team of volunteers dedicated to keeping the line and locomotives going. He also gets a rare glimpse inside the radar base at RAF Fylingdales, a missile-tracking facility that plays a significant role in the defence of both the UK and the USA. In Whitby, Tony visits the area's last shipyard and is delighted to hear that whales have returned to local waters after hunting almost drove them to extinction. His final destination is the picturesque coastal village of Robin Hood's Bay(n)

programme.title: 70 Stone: The Man Who Couldn't Be Saved
programme.desc: Documentary from 2013 following Keith Martin, who weighs 70 stone and has not left his home for 11 years, as he enlists obesity specialists at the Homerton University Hospital in east London to help prepare him for life-saving surgery. The team quickly realises that operating immediately is not an option, so puts him on an eight-month diet and exercise plan to give the procedure the best chance of success(n)

programme.title: 70 Stone: The Man Who Couldn't Be Saved

The film features footage of the surgery and interviews with specialists, exploring the case from the point of view of the medical staff as well as Keith himself(n)

programme.desc: Engineer Rob Bell tells the story behind the design and construction of Isambard Kingdom Brunel's famous bridge over the picturesque Avon gorge at Bristol, and discovers that some people question whether it was entirely the product of his own devising. The bridge remained unfinished in Brunel's lifetime, but when he died in 1859, his friends decided that the bridge should be completed as a memorial, using the chains from another of his suspension bridges that was being demolished(n)

programme.title: Paddington Station 24/7
programme.desc: A vital piece of track-insulating equipment has failed, throwing train services into chaos, while a nasty surprise awaits engineers as they discover rotten wooden sleepers that disintegrate under foot. Plus, veteran staff are shocked by the reason the 15:00 to Bristol has come to a halt - a driver has tried to start the train from the wrong end, leading it to automatically shut down(n)

programme.title: 5 News at Lunchtime
programme.desc: Round-up of the day's headlines from around the world(n)

programme.title: GPs: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: The doctors and nurses of Horfield Health Centre turn their attention to patients dealing with persistent pain, both physical and psychological. First up is Valentine, who is undergoing male-to-female gender reassignment, but faces a long and arduous journey to her desired destination. Dr Tom Pelly helps Vera, who is worried that her bad shoulder might be a sign of worse things to come, and Dr Alison Bolam solves the mystery of why visually impaired patient David is experiencing pain in his eyes and face. Dr Jane Edge believe impetigo might be the cause of Agnese's woes, and Dr Elizabeth Barnard suspects Hannah's abdominal pains might be something far more serious than a case of gastroenteritis(n)

programme.title: Access
programme.desc: Showbiz news and gossip(n)

programme.title: Home and Away
